name: factor-correlations
invariants: output.result.hasData === false || output.result.correlations.every(c => typeof c.delta === 'number' && Number.isFinite(c.delta))
_note: mental-health.factor-correlations (Daylio-style) computes, per user-defined activity factor, the average tagged-mood delta vs the user's baseline, labelling each factor lifts/lowers/neutral. It is degrade-graceful: below minSamples it returns ok:true with hasData:false and an empty correlations array (never throws — wrapped in try/catch). minSamples is clamped to >=2 so a poisoned/absurd value can't break bucketing; deltas are finite rounded numbers derived only from real logged moods (no fabricated correlation data). Pinned by server/tests/mental-health-lens-macros.test.js. Lens-action domain — not assassin-enumerable via runMacro.
